channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Pakistani Mediators Arrive in Iran to Keep Peace Talks Alive
item.description: The diplomacy came as the White House dismissed reports that President Trump wanted to extend the cease-fire, while still expressing optimism that talks could yield an agreement.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Catholicism Thrives in Africa, but Pope’s Cameroon Visit Highlights a Divide
item.description: Catholicism is growing fast on the continent, yet Africans play a comparatively small role in church leadership. Cameroon, where Leo XIV arrived on Wednesday, shows the disparity.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich
item.media:credit: Daniel Beloumou Olomo/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Pope Leo arriving in Cameroon’s capital, Yaoundé, on Tuesday.

item.title: How China’s Weapons Transfers to Iran Have Evolved Over Decades
item.description: China has denied claims this month by U.S. intelligence that it might have shipped arms to Iran. In recent years, China has supported Iran with dual-use parts.
item.dc:creator: David Pierson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Military delegates at an annual legislative session in Beijing last month.

item.title: Dodging Bombs and Drones, This Postman in Ukraine Still Delivers
item.description: Oleksiy Klochkovsky has driven mail and parcels around the front line in Ukraine for four years. He keeps one ear tuned for danger from above.
item.dc:creator: Cassandra Vinograd and Oleksandr Chubko
item.media:credit: Brendan Hoffman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Oleksiy Klochkovsky, a driver for Nova Poshta, a private postal service in Ukraine, in the back of his truck in the Kharkiv region in January.

item.title: Claudia Sheinbaum’s War on Crime in Mexico Faces a Grim Reckoning: 133,000 Missing People
item.description: President Claudia Sheinbaum of Mexico is under pressure to end one of her country’s most painful tragedies: the disappearance of more than 133,000 people.
item.dc:creator: Emiliano Rodríguez Mega and Paulina Villegas
item.media:credit: Fred Ramos for The New York Times
item.media:description: Izaguirre ranch in Teuchitlán, Jalisco, where volunteer groups found charred human remains. The Jalisco New Generation Cartel had been using it as a training camp for its new recruits.

item.title: When El Salvador’s Prisoners Are Set Free, She’s There To Pick Them Up
item.description: Sugey Amaya’s brother was detained in El Salvador’s mass arrest campaign four years ago. She has devoted her life to helping prisoners like him.
item.dc:creator: Fred Ramos and Annie Correal
item.media:credit: Fred Ramos for The New York Times
item.media:description: Sugey Amaya has spent the past three years waiting for her brother, Alexis, to be released from prison. He was detained under President Nayib Bukele’s state of emergency and accused of being part of a gang.

item.title: Trump Breaks With Meloni, Italy’s Leader, Amid Dispute Over Pope and Iran
item.description: Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni was once considered one of President Trump’s closest European allies. Their friendship now appears in danger.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump greeting Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni of Italy at the White House last year. They were once considered close allies.

item.title: Top U.S. Diplomat in Venezuela Leaves Post After Arriving in January
item.description: Laura Dogu, who reopened the U.S. Embassy in Venezuela, returns to advising Gen. Dan Caine, the highest ranking U.S. military officer, as the war with Iran continues.
item.dc:creator: Simon Romero
item.media:description: Laura Dogu, the top U.S. diplomat in Venezuela, last month in the capital, Caracas.

item.title: With Iran War on Pause, Political Battle Resumes Over Israeli Democracy
item.description: A court fight over Itamar Ben-Gvir, an extremist who oversees the police, pits the attorney general against Benjamin Netanyahu and his right-wing government.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger
item.media:description: Israeli Supreme Court justices on Wednesday before hearing a petition to force Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to oust the national security minister, Itamar Ben-Gvir.

item.title: ‘We Cannot Lose Sight of Ukraine.’ Europeans Promise More Aid to Kyiv.
item.description: The focus in recent weeks has been on the conflict in Iran, but European leaders said it was crucial to keep aid flowing to Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Steven Erlanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Mark Rutte, NATO’s secretary general, met with the defense ministers of Ukraine, Germany and Britain in Berlin on Wednesday.

item.title: After Fighting for the Rights of Workers, Brazil’s President Lula Faces a Labor Abuse Crisis
item.description: The dismissal of Brazil’s top labor inspector has landed the leftist government of President Lula, a former union leader, in an awkward spot.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ionova
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:credit: Victor Moriyama for The New York Times
item.media:description: A factory for the automaker BYD under construction last year in Camaçari, Bahia, Brazil. A labor ministry inspection found that Chinese workers building the plant were living in squalid conditions.

item.title: American Streamer Johnny Somali Is Sentenced to Prison in South Korea
item.description: Known for desecrating a monument to victims of sexual slavery, the former YouTuber was convicted on multiple charges and given a six-month sentence.
item.dc:creator: Jin Yu Young
item.media:credit: Yonhap, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Johnny Somali, an American YouTuber, arriving at a Seoul courthouse on Wednesday. He was sentenced to six months in jail.

item.title: At Least 4 Dead in Second School Shooting in Turkey in 2 Days
item.description: An armed eighth grader opened fire in his middle school. One of those killed was a teacher.
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard and Safak Timur
item.media:description: An ambulance leaving the scene after a deadly school shooting in southern Turkey on Wednesday.

item.title: Kuwait Detained Journalist After Comments on Iran War Images, Watchdog Says
item.description: The country has not commented publicly about Ahmed Shihab-Eldin, who the Committee to Protect Journalists said had been charged with spreading false information.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Ahmed Shihab‑Eldin in Qatar in November. He has not posted online or been seen in public since early March.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado, Aaron Boxerman and Erika Solomon

item.title: 3 Killed in Boat Strike in the Pacific, Pentagon Says
item.description: It was the third such attack by the U.S. military in three days, and the 51st attack in a campaign against people who the United States has accused of smuggling drugs.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US Strikes on Alleged Drug Traffickers (2025- )
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Francis L. Donovan at a confirmation hearing in January to become the leader of U.S. Southern Command.

item.title: China’s G.D.P. Stronger Than Expected, Led by Infrastructure Spending
item.description: A steep slide in housing prices has left consumers less prosperous and less willing to spend, but the government is pouring money into new rail lines and other projects.
item.dc:creator: Keith Bradsher
item.media:description: An import and export trade show in southern China this week.

item.title: Iran Will Be in U.S. for World Cup, FIFA’s Infantino Says
item.description: The comments come as peace talks between Iran and the United States have not yet resumed and a cease-fire is set to expire next week.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.media:description: Iran tried unsuccessfully to move its matches from the United States to Mexico.

item.title: Shakespeare Bought One Property in London. Now We Know Exactly Where.
item.description: In confirming the precise location of William Shakespeare’s Blackfriars house, a British scholar raises fresh questions about what he intended to do with it.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.media:credit: Jeremie Souteyrat for The New York Times
item.media:description: William Shakespeare’s property was just across the river from the Globe Theater, left, which staged his plays then and does so today, having been recreated and reopened in 1997.

item.title: Sudan Enters Fourth Year of War Amid World’s Most Severe Humanitarian Crisis
item.description: Fighting that erupted in 2023 has forced millions from their homes and left widespread hunger that is now being compounded by strife in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni and Declan Walsh
item.media:description: A 2-month-old patient, Saidal Altaher, getting treatment for malnutrition on Wednesday in Port Sudan, Sudan.

item.title: Trump’s Portrayal of the War in Iran Collides With Reality
item.description: President Trump is confronting a crisis that is not bending to his narrative of a “pretty reasonable” new regime in Iran and all-but-assured victory for the United States.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump in Miami last week. Mr. Trump has declared a “regime change” accomplishment in Iran, even though analysts believe the war may have only increased the internal sway of Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps.

item.title: U.K.’s Starmer Summons U.S. Social Media Companies Over Child Safety Online
item.description: The British prime minister will meet with executives from Meta, Google and other companies on Thursday as his government explores how to protect children from online harms.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain speaking about online safety at a school in London last month.

item.title: Iran could struggle to enforce all of its latest shipping threats, experts say.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.media:description: A vessel in the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday. Traffic is still at a fraction of its usual levels.

item.title: The Pope in Africa
item.description: The continent is crucial to the future of the church, but it’s also the center of long-running tensions over some of Catholicism’s most charged issues.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.media:credit: Daniel Beloumou Olomo/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Pope Leo XIV arrived in Cameroon’s capital, Yaoundé, yesterday.

item.title: Pakistani Mediators Arrive in Tehran in Bid to Keep U.S.-Iran Peace Talks Alive
item.description: The diplomacy came as the U.S. Navy locked down trade to Iranian ports, and Iran responded by threatening critical shipping routes across the region.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier, Eric Schmitt, Jenny Gross and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A vessel at the Strait of Hormuz, off the coast of Oman’s Musandam province, on Sunday.

item.title: Saudi Fund to Back Away From LIV Golf Under Mounting Financial Pressures
item.description: The Saudi league, established in 2022, attracted some of the sport’s biggest stars with huge contracts.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Hirsch, Vivian Nereim and Alan Blinder
item.media:description: Yasir al-Rumayyan, left, the head of Saudi Arabia’s sovereign wealth fund, presenting a trophy at the LIV Golf championship in Michigan in 2025.

item.title: Getting the Pope to Answer a Pointed Question at 30,000 Feet
item.description: The Times’s Rome bureau chief is still getting a feel for a pontiff who is willing to challenge President Trump and use his sense of humor.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Pope Leo XIV, flanked by press minders, spoke to journalists during a flight to Algeria on Monday.

item.title: South Africa Names Roelf Meyer, Apartheid-Era Negotiator, as U.S. Ambassador
item.description: The move comes as the country looks to strengthen diplomatic ties with the United States after months of hostility between the two nations.
item.dc:creator: Zimasa Matiwane
item.media:description: Roelf Meyer, center, with President Cyril Ramaphosa of South Africa to his right at an event in Pretoria in August. Mr. Meyer was appointed ambassador to the United States on Wednesday.

item.title: BBC Announces Plan to Cut Around 2,000 Jobs
item.description: The cuts amount to about 10 percent of the work force at the British public broadcaster, which is facing financial pressures.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia
item.media:description: The headquarters of the British Broadcasting Corporation in London.

item.title: Debt Alarms Ring as Countries Rack Up More Emergency Spending
item.description: Amid signs of a prolonged period of high energy costs, policymakers are urging restraint as governments open up the public till to protect households and businesses.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson
item.media:description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz of Germany, second from left, with other officials at a news briefing in Berlin this week.

item.title: Israel Considering Cease-Fire With Lebanon, Officials Say
item.description: Talk of a truce that would pause the war between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon followed rare direct talks between Israel and Lebanon in Washington on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ronen Bergman, Aaron Boxerman and Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Residents collecting scrap metal from a power station destroyed by Israeli airstrikes in Tyre, Lebanon, earlier this month. While Israel recently scaled back attacks on Beirut, the Lebanese capital, it has continued striking the southern part of the country.

item.title: Iran-Linked Ships Slow or Stop as U.S. Navy Enforces Strait of Hormuz Blockade
item.description: More than a dozen U.S. Navy warships are enforcing a blockade on all vessels from all nations entering or leaving coastal areas or ports in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A vessel in the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday. Traffic is still at a fraction of its usual levels.

item.title: In Defense of Dumb Dogs
item.description: Your pet is (probably) not a genius, and that’s OK.
item.dc:creator: Emily Anthes

item.title: Here’s how the U.S. blockade of Iranian shipping is taking shape.
item.description: The military’s blockade of Iranian ports aims to suppress sea traffic to and from Iran, while leaving the strait itself open.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon
item.media:description: A cargo ship off the coast of Ras al-Khaimah, United Arab Emirates, on Monday.

item.title: Kanye West Postpones Concert in France as Government Considers Banning It
item.description: The rapper formerly known as Kanye West had been scheduled to perform in Marseille, France, in June. The city’s mayor had said he did not want it to be “a showcase for those who promote hatred.”
item.dc:creator: Claire Moses
item.media:description: Ye announced that he had postponed a concert in Marseille, France.

item.title: U.S. Applies Little Pressure to Israel to Stop the Fighting in Lebanon
item.description: Despite hailing negotiations between the two sides as a “historic milestone,” Washington did not call for a halt to Israeli strikes or for a withdrawal from Lebanese territory.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sánchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mourning victims of airstrikes at a temporary cemetery in Aamrousiyeh, Lebanon, on Monday. Hezbollah flags are displayed at some graves.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Erika Solomon, Tyler Pager, Karoun Demirjian and Pranav Baskar

item.title: How a Times Reporter Has Covered a Turbulent Period in Latin America
item.description: Frances Robles, an international correspondent for The New York Times, has reported on the region for more than 25 years.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr
item.category: Coups D'Etat and Attempted Coups D'Etat
item.media:description: The reporter Frances Robles, right, with the Rev. Thomas Ngigi at St. Theresa’s parish, a waterfront church in Black River, Jamaica, that was badly damaged by Hurricane Melissa last year.

item.title: Super Typhoon Sinlaku Slams Saipan, Causing Widespread Damage
item.description: But there were no early reports of casualties from the storm, which was named Sinlaku and was moving northwest of the region.
item.dc:creator: Laura Chung
item.media:description: Debris scattered on Wednesday in Saipan, Northern Mariana Islands, as the super typhoon Sinlaku moved through.

item.title: Ship ‘Spoofing’ in Strait of Hormuz May Compound Confusion
item.description: A new pattern of deceptive activity by some vessels around the critical waterway suggests the new American blockade is changing how some ships linked to Iran are behaving.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A cargo ship off the coast of Ras al-Khaimah, United Arab Emirates, on Monday.

item.title: Rubio Hosts Israel and Lebanon for Rare Meeting Shadowed by U.S.-Iran War
item.description: The gathering ended with encouraging words, even as Israel continued to refuse to halt its military campaign against Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley and Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio meeting with officials from Lebanon and Israel at the State Department in Washington on Tuesday.

item.title: Trump’s Blockade Risks Upending an Emerging Détente With China
item.description: In a thinly veiled critique of the war in Iran, China’s leader said the world could not risk reverting “to the law of the jungle.”
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger and Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: President Trump meeting with China’s leader, Xi Jinping, in South Korea last October. They will meet again next month.

item.title: Carney Suspends Gas Tax in Canada as War Drives Up Fuel Costs
item.description: The prime minister said that his government was also investing in ways to boost oil and gas production.
item.dc:creator: Matina Stevis-Gridneff
item.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney of Canada at a news conference in Ottawa, on Tuesday.

item.title: Trump Says 2nd Round of U.S.-Iran Talks Could Take Place This Week
item.description: The president did not say who would represent the United States in a potential next round of direct talks, though he ruled out being personally involved.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump at the White House on Monday.

item.title: Mario Ríos Montt, Bishop and Brother of Genocidal General, Dies at 94
item.description: In Guatemala, he led the Catholic Church’s human rights office. His brother, a dictator who took power in a coup, was convicted of crimes against humanity.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nossiter
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:description: Bishop Mario Ríos Montt in 1999 at a news conference on human-rights atrocities committed by the military in Guatemala. “What interests me is the truth, and come what may, the truth is the truth,” he said.

item.title: Rare Direct Talks Between Israel and Lebanon Focus on Hezbollah
item.description: A meeting between Israeli and Lebanese officials in Washington came as the U.S. blockade of Iranian ports entered its second day.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley, Euan Ward, Anushka Patil and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio, center, speaking during a meeting with Lebanese and Israeli ambassadors at the State Department on Tuesday.

item.title: Hungary’s Populist Paradox
item.description: We look at what Viktor Orban’s defeat tells us about a model of government in which politics are more important than the economy.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: King Charles III and Queen Camilla Will Visit D.C., New York and Virginia
item.description: The planned four-day state visit comes at a fraught time in the U.S.-U.K. relationship, following President Trump’s frequent belittling of the British prime minister.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
Camilla, Queen Consort of the United Kingdom
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Queen Camilla and King Charles III of Britain earlier this month.

item.title: Israel’s Spy Chief Addresses Criticism About the War With Iran
item.description: The Mossad head said Israel’s mission would not be complete until the Iranian regime was replaced, after criticism that the war has left Iran’s theocratic government in place.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner and Gabby Sobelman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tehran on Saturday.

item.title: Russian Oil Revenues Nearly Doubled in March
item.description: The surge in revenue provided a critical lifeline for Moscow, which has struggled to fund the war in Ukraine amid record-high deficits.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Nechepurenko
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A Panamanian-flagged ship carrying crude oil from Russia, moored off an oil refinery in Limay, Bataan Province, the Philippines, this month.

item.title: How Peter Magyar Defeated Viktor Orban, a Former Ally, In Hungary’s Election
item.description: For years, Peter Magyar was a loyal ally of Viktor Orban, the far-right Hungarian leader. Then he changed sides — and defeated his former boss in a landslide victory on Sunday. Does he represent real change?
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins and Lili Rutai
item.media:credit: Akos Stiller for The New York Times
item.media:description: Peter Magyar campaigning in 2024 in Viktor Orban’s hometown, Felcsut, Hungary.

item.title: Benin’s Romuald Wadagni Poised to Win Presidential Election
item.description: Romuald Wadagni received 94 percent of the vote, according to provisional results. He faces the challenge of a growing jihadist insurgency in the region.
item.dc:creator: Saikou Jammeh
item.category: Coups D'Etat and Attempted Coups D'Etat
Economic Community of West African States (Ecowas)
item.media:description: Romuald Wadagni campaigning in Cotonou, Benin, last month. He has become the country’s new president, according to provisional results.

item.title: Italy Suspends Defense Pact With Israel, Further Straining Ties
item.description: The move by Italy, once considered a strong ally of Israel, reflected growing anger over Israeli aggression in the Middle East. President Trump criticized Italy’s stance on the war.
item.dc:creator: Elisabetta Povoledo
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni in Rome last week.

item.title: China Offers Veiled Criticism of U.S. as Iran War Threatens Oil Imports
item.description: China’s leader, Xi Jinping, said that the world cannot risk reverting “to the law of the jungle.” Beijing has taken a more active role diplomatically as the crisis in the Strait of Hormuz persists.
item.dc:creator: Lily Kuo
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: China’s leader Xi Jinping has tried to maintain a neutral stance on the war in Iran as it balances its partnership with Tehran and managing tensions with the United States.

item.title: Middle East War Will Slow Global Economic Growth, I.M.F. Warns
item.description: The conflict could also fuel another bout of inflation, according to the International Monetary Fund.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: New International Monetary Fund projections showed a global economy that was stopped in its tracks by the U.S. war in Iran.

item.title: State of U.S. Blockade Is Unclear as Some Ships Transit Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Ship-tracking data showed that several vessels, including some that had been docked at Iranian ports, had moved through the strait as the U.S. military began its blockade.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon and Francesca Regalado
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: A ship is seen in the Persian Gulf off the coast of Sharjah, United Arab Emirates, on Monday, the day after U.S.-Iran peace talks broke down.

item.title: Will Viktor Orban’s Legacy Live On in Brussels, Even Without Him?
item.description: Viktor Orban, Hungary’s newly ousted prime minister, helped to finance a Brussels think tank that pushes his populist vision. It will outlast him, at least for a while.
item.dc:creator: Jeanna Smialek and Koba Ryckewaert
item.media:description: Viktor Orban at an European Union summit meeting in Brussels, in March.

item.title: Ex-Student Wounds 16 in High School Shooting in Turkey
item.description: Officials said the teenage attacker killed himself when cornered by the police. The authorities are investigating possible motives.
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard and Safak Timur
item.media:description: Security forces outside a high school in Siverek, southeastern Turkey, on Tuesday, after a shooting on the premises.
